title: get() single-key path should use .into_values().next() not .into_iter().next()
description: lib.rs:129: let (_, bs) = data.into_iter().next().unwrap() — the key is discarded. Idiomatic: data.into_values().next(). Avoids destructuring a tuple to throw away half, and signals intent (we want the value, not the key). BTreeMap::into_values() is stable since Rust 1.54.
